summary: Decent soundboard capture of a lengthy May ’78 show with solid performances like “They Love Each Other” and “Wharf Rat”, though tracks such as “Bertha” fall flat.
review: The recording is sourced from the lai.3899 sound‑board tape, generally clear but with the matrix mix sounding better on “Bertha”. Musically the show sits in the middle of the road – highlights include a fluid “Me & My Uncle”, an uptempo “It Must Have Been the Roses” and a jam‑filled “Wharf Rat”, while early second‑set numbers like “Bertha” and a sloppy “Sugar Magnolia” drag it down. Overall sentiment from reviewers is mixed, reflecting both strong moments and noticeable unevenness.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers note a few standout songs and an overall decent soundboard source, but also point out several weak spots; the average star rating (≈2) is lifted by detailed positive comments, yielding a modest 3‑star AI rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: The lai.3899 SBD is praised as the best source; however the matrix mix is preferred for “Bertha”. Overall clarity is good, with no major drop‑outs reported.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been the Roses
show_quality.setlist_flow: A lengthy May ’78 show with a nearly two‑hour second set; after a sluggish start on “Bertha” the band picks up momentum through “It Must Have Been the Roses”, the Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World sequence, and culminates in a strong “Wharf Rat” jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fluid lead on “Me & My Uncle”; scrumptious solo on “Big River”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers chorded “bombs” during the “Wharf Rat” jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming described as long and monotonous, especially during the extended “Space”.
